description: In C, the toolchain triple, libc version, and compiler version are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In C, the toolchain triple, libc version, and compiler version define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the toolchain triple, libc version, and compiler version or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the toolchain triple, libc version, and compiler version, then run clang-tidy, cppcheck, and -fanalyzer warnings and make / Makefile targets (and the exact CC, CFLAGS, and -I/-L flags) to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical clang-tidy, cppcheck, and -fanalyzer warnings output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
